statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget.
The system shall alert administrators to budget violations.
The system shall allow the IFA to audit team budgets and set regulatory policies.
The system shall allow IFA administrators to view detailed financial transactions.
The system shall allow the referee to insert game events in real time.
The system shall allow the insertion of game events only after the game has started.
The system shall allow the aggregation and export of seasonal statistics such as red cards and goals per team for distribution to external parties such as journalists or press.
The system shall allow unregistered users to access aggregated season data such as number of red cards and goals per team.
The system shall allow the aggregated data to be accessible to all users.
The system shall make financial data accessible to all members within the IFA and the finance department.
The system shall restrict budget transaction entry to individual teams, with each team only able to access and modify their own data.
The system shall allow the IFA to view the entire monetary information.
The system shall allow alerting and requesting details for adjustment but shall not perform operations on the scheduling data.
The system shall have the ability to define policies for scheduling games.
The system shall provide guidance for allocating referees to games based on defined policies.
The system shall ensure that the same referee is not assigned to multiple games involving the same teams.
The system shall facilitate the definition of referee assignment policies and automatically assign referees to games based on those policies.
The system shall enable referees to set their availability or preferences for games.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall allow manual override of scheduled results under certain circumstances.
The system shall allow automatic scheduling and override by the IFA representative.
The system shall incorporate a scheduling policy that ensures a minimum gap of twice the number of teams in days between matches for any team.
The system shall send push email notifications to fans when their registered favorite player posts a message.
The system shall allow users to subscribe to each type of information mentioned and receive notifications accordingly.
The system shall include all functionality within the application or website.
The system shall provide different interfaces within the same system for various user roles or functions.
The system shall allow only referees to update game data.
The system shall provide all necessary game data directly, eliminating the need for stakeholders to consult external news portals.
The system shall allow users to specify alerts, such as a reminder five minutes before the game starts.
The system shall provide a response to the referee within a reasonable time frame.
The system shall support functionality for fans, IFA administration, and team administration.
The system shall enable real-time communication with referees during games.
The system shall be highly responsive and prioritize referee communications over other stadium activities.
The system shall support a live recording feature, possibly including input from a side referee.
The system shall facilitate communication between the software development team and the IFA through the organization's IT Department.
The system shall be fully managed by an external company, with no operational involvement from the in-house IT department.
The system shall have all storage and computing resources managed by an external cloud service provider after deployment.
The system shall be adjustable to comply with local regulations and local review requirements.
The system shall comply with data residency restrictions imposed by certain countries.
The system shall support configuration of data center locations for labeling and allocation purposes.
The system shall make all data accessible to everyone except for budget data, which shall be restricted.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent unauthorized access or data leakage.
The system shall allow players to update their social network page.
The system shall support team registration, league setup, season configuration, referee registration, and fan registration as core functionalities.
The system shall support budgeting and scheduling functionalities in the second phase.
The system shall allow the referee to update game information in real time.
The system shall allow team registration via the internet to enable identification.
The system shall allow team managers or owners to submit a new team registration with valid documents, which can be approved by the IFA.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall retain team, referee, and player registration data.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues, define seasons, appoint or register referees, and set policies for budgets and scheduling.
The system shall allow referees to be appointed by the IFA.
The system shall allow a representative to invite a referee to join the system.
The system shall allow the team to enter player details into the system.
The system shall allow the IFA to audit the referee and player registration process.
The system shall maintain a complete audit log of every action performed by IFA administrators.
The system shall provide a financial summary dashboard for the IFA to view key budget metrics across all teams.
The system shall provide a public API for journalists to programmatically access game data in a later version.
The system shall allow referees to enter match events into the application in real time during the match.
The system shall automatically notify any fan who has registered to follow a specific game if the schedule for that game is changed.
The system shall display a live standings table showing current league rankings during the season.
The system shall allow fans to compare player statistics side by side.
The system shall perform daily backups of all data.
The system shall require administrator accounts to use a second form of verification before granting access.
The system shall allow fans to filter upcoming fixtures by team or by date.
The system shall support multiple languages.
The system shall allow features to be considered for future implementation if deemed beneficial but not critical.
The system shall allow referees to flag disputed events for review.
The system shall allow each team to independently record and manage its own financial income and expenditure.
The system shall automatically generate the season schedule for each league at the start of the season.
The system shall allow the IFA to set the rules governing how games are scheduled.
The system shall require an IFA administrator to document the reason for modifying a generated schedule and obtain authorisation from a different IFA administrator before the change is applied.
The system shall be capable of handling a minimum of fifty thousand concurrent active users without performance degradation.
The system shall require users to create an account and sign in to access functionality beyond read-only mode.
The system shall provide read-only guest access to users who have not registered.
The system shall alert the IFA when a team's recorded salary expenditure exceeds the wage cap defined in the IFA budget policy.
The system shall record the exact minute and the identity of the scorer when a goal is logged during a match.
The system shall prevent the assignment of a referee to a team's match if that referee officiated the same team in the immediately preceding round.
The system shall allow a team administrator to register a newly signed player by entering the player's personal details and position.
The system shall display each team's home and away match records separately within the league standings.
The system shall display a top goal-scorer leaderboard for the current season.
The system shall allow IFA administrators to generate a financial summary report for any specified date range.
The system shall allow team administrators to attach supporting documents such as invoices or contracts to individual budget transactions.
The system shall display the venue and kick-off time for every scheduled match.
